title: Rankett 12-Month Business Plan — $100k/mo target, 3-tier ladder, hard non-negotiables
html:
Rankett 12-Month Business Plan — $100k/mo target, 3-tier ladder, hard non-negotiables

Source: 2025-12-19 Google Doc. The Rankett north-star plan — written ~2 weeks after the direct offer doc and 1 month before the Diddles call. Sets the $100k/mo MRR target and the 3-tier product ladder (Direct / Reseller / Platform).

TL;DR

  • 12-month target: $100,000/month MRR across services, partners, and SaaS by end of cycle.
  • Product ladder:
    • Core Offer (direct) — done-for-you AI/SEO visibility engine for high-LTV owners at $3,500/mo.
    • Reseller — solo marketers/small agencies (20-50+ clients) bringing partners; %-based: Rankett bills end-client, marketer gets 20-30% Y1 + optional 10-20% ongoing.
    • Platform Access (SaaS) — self-serve scan + insights, "tier-1 / Walmart level: cheap, simple, self-guided."
  • Sequencing:
    • Months 0-3: close 3-5 Core Offer clients ($3,500/mo).
    • Months 3-6: launch reseller program, recruit 3-10 partners.
    • Months 6+: launch SaaS.
  • ICPs defined:
    • Primary: high-LTV local/regional businesses ($20k+ LTV per client).
    • Multiplier: solo marketers / small agencies with 20-50+ clients.
    • Secondary (opportunistic only): marketing directors at $5-10M+ companies via warm partner intros.
  • Core problem statement: "You're invisible or under-represented when buyers ask AI and Google who to hire."
  • Hard non-negotiables (worth quoting):
    • No legwork for SaaS clients (docs + self-serve only).
    • No C-tier clients (low-LTV, can't afford $3,500/mo).
    • No custom builds for one partner (custom waits until multiple request the same).
    • No confusing the price ladder (Lambo always most expensive, SaaS always cheapest).
    • No multi-month unpaid pilots.
    • No channel conflict (don't poach partner clients).
    • No feature bloat without revenue validation.
    • No pivoting without clear kill metrics — "If the model isn't hitting $100K/month trajectory by month 9, reassess; don't keep pushing a dead model."
  • Financial mix: Lambo 15-20 clients × $3,500 = $52,500-70,000/mo + partner revenue (Rankett keeps 70-80%) + SaaS to fill gap.

Pattern observed

This is Yahir's most disciplined founder document in the corpus. The non-negotiables section in particular is 9 hard rules with zero hedging language — a sharp contrast to the 2024 07 mega notebook uncertainty about post-college direction. By Dec 2025, the Rankett strategy has clarified into something almost crystalline.

The ladder vocabulary (Lambo / Fred Meyer / Walmart for the three tiers) is a memorable internal naming — premium done-for-you, mid-tier reseller, cheap self-serve. Same naming-to-anchor-quality logic he uses elsewhere.

The 12-month plan partially evolves by April 2026: the SaaS tier disappears, the partner program expands and gets a $299/mo platform fee, free tier gets killed entirely. The non-negotiables hold; the structure tightens.

Open questions

  • The Month-9 reassess-trigger ($100k MRR trajectory check) — is Rankett on pace as of vault creation?
  • Has Yahir hit any of the Core Offer 3-5 clients at $3,500/mo? (Visible: free-work clients Scott Thorson and Caspian per 2026 04 27 rankett com build doc — those are PoCs, not paying clients.)
  • The 30% partner-revenue share — has any partner actually been paid out yet?
